name: AEVEX Corp.
business: AEVEX designs, manufactures, and supports autonomous unmanned systems (UxS), including UAS and USV, and provides full-spectrum ISR, C-UAS, additive manufacturing, and aircraft modification services. Its solutions are sold primarily to the U.S. Department of War, Special Operations Forces, Intelligence Community, and allied international partners.
sellsTo: California Fire Office of Emergency Services
namedCustomers: Program Executive Office for Intelligence, Surveillance, and Reconnaissance / SOF
California Fire Office of Emergency Services

exposures.sentence: We believe that we are a leading defense technology prime contractor and critical enabler of U.S. UAS dominance strategy missions.
Our UxS portfolio is underpinned by our proprietary AI-based CompassX sensor-fusion engine, which delivers APNT and advanced onboard autonomy in GPS-denied environments.

Global Solutions: Provides bespoke mission solutions, including AI-enabled full-spectrum airborne ISR, C-UAS, additive manufacturing, and specialized mission aircraft engineering, modification, and testing.

this holistic ecosystem provides resilient and all-domain visual-based navigation capabilities by combining dual wide-field-of-view (FOV) cameras with visual odometry to deliver GPS-independent positioning, day or night, in contested environments.

Additionally, ForgeX is our highly innovative, deployable, additive manufacturing solution, which enables resiliency on the frontlines of the battlefield by manufacturing mission-ready UxS at the point of need.
Electronic Warfare Solutions Advanced EW Capabilities designed to provide a strategic advantage through a cutting edge, family of systems in the high order fight with peer and near-peer adversaries.

Global Solutions: Provides bespoke mission solutions, including AI-enabled full-spectrum airborne ISR, C-UAS, additive manufacturing, and specialized mission aircraft engineering, modification, and testing.

catalysts.sentence: On April 20, 2026, we completed an IPO of shares of our Class A common stock.
On April 20, 2026, Holdings LLC refinanced the Prior Credit Facilities and entered into the New Credit Facilities.
99% growth in our funded backlog from $179.2 million as of December 31, 2024, to over $356.6 million as of March 31, 2026.
approximately 100.0% increase in our pipeline of identified growth opportunities from $4.1 billion as of December 2024 to $8.2 billion as of March 2026.

catalysts.event: Systems committed through end of 2026: over 3,900
catalysts.sentence: We have delivered over 6,200 UxS and have over 3,900 more systems committed through the end of 2026.

tamClaims.claim: U.S. core UxS TAM in 2030: ~$11.0 billion annually
tamClaims.sentence: The U.S. 2030 total addressable market for UASs, launched effects, loitering munitions, one-way attack systems and USVs is expected to reach approximately $11.0 billion annually.

tamClaims.claim: Global core UxS TAM in 2030: ~$26.0 billion annually
tamClaims.sentence: the total global addressable market in 2030 for these systems is expected to contribute approximately $26.0 billion in total annual opportunities.

tamClaims.claim: U.S. adjacent UxS TAM in 2030: ~$19.0 billion annually
tamClaims.sentence: adjacent market addressable opportunity is expected to reach approximately $19.0 billion annually by 2030 for the U.S.

tamClaims.claim: Combined U.S. TAM in 2030: ~$31.0 billion annually
tamClaims.sentence: Combined, the complete addressable annual opportunity for the U.S is expected to reach approximately $31.0 billion by 2030.

tamClaims.claim: Combined global TAM in 2030: ~$49.0 billion annually
tamClaims.sentence: the global opportunity reaching approximately $49.0 billion.
